method_atom: constructing discrete complex-analytic (discrete-holomorphic) observables on a broad family of planar discretizations of a critical statistical system, and proving that as the mesh is refined these observables converge, under appropriate boundary conditions on bounded domains, to continuum holomorphic functions; this establishes that the fine-scale limit is universal (independent of the microscopic lattice structure) and invariant under angle-preserving (conformal) coordinate transformations, upgrading a weaker Mobius/scale symmetry to full conformal symmetry
object_atom: the two-dimensional Ising model at its critical point — binary spins with nearest-neighbor interactions placed on planar graphs — together with its fermionic correlation observables
brief_reason: the transferable part is the discrete-holomorphicity machinery that forces a critical lattice model's scaling limit to be universal and conformally invariant; the domain-specific part is the 2D critical Ising spin system and its fermionic observables.
